title: hydrocotyle rivularis
intro: Hydrocotyle rivularis, also known as Pennywort or Indian pennywort, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Asia, characterized by its rounded leaves and stems that grow in water or moist soil. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Hydrocotyle rivularis include reducing stress and anxiety, improving cognitive function, and supporting liver and kidney health, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and cholesterol. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a powder to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
